summary:
Frontend-only retest of the new CMS structured editors + TipTap rich-text pipeline. All 6 CMS page cards render, HomeEditor exposes every requested testid (cms-tagline, cms-hero-headline, cms-kanji, cms-hero-sub, cms-intro with rte-root toolbar). End-to-end confirmed: (1) Edit /home -> tagline changed to 'TEST CMS UPDATE', bold word added in intro via rte-btn-bold, Save closes modal; public / page shows new tagline and BOLDWORD rendered through RichContent's dangerouslySetInnerHTML. (2) NewsEditor 'Add' prepends a new post row (3->4), title+bullet-list body saved; public /news shows 'TEST POST' with a
  • list. (3) ProgramsEditor add (4->5), reorder (row-up-1 moved row 1 into slot 0 - content swap verified), remove (5->4) all work locally. (4) ScheduleEditor add + edit last class name, Save, public /schedule shows 'TEST CLASS MARKER'. (5) Advanced details panel prints raw JSON containing and 'TEST CMS UPDATE'. All CMS pages (home, news, programs, schedule) RESTORED to DEFAULT_PAGES seed via authenticated PUT /api/cms/pages/{slug} with yk_token Bearer; verified afterwards by GET /api/cms/pages/home (tagline='Traditional Shotokan Karate', original intro text present). Backend not re-run in this iteration - iteration_3 already locked in 42/42 pytest pass.
action_items: LOW: TipTap console warning 'Duplicate extension names found: ["link"]'. StarterKit already bundles its own Link; adding @tiptap/extension-link again triggers the dup warning. Either remove StarterKit's built-in link via StarterKit.configure({ link: false }) or drop the separate Link import in RichTextEditor.jsx.

LOW: RichContent renders CMS HTML with dangerouslySetInnerHTML without server-side sanitisation. Today only super_admins can write it, but if that ever loosens consider running DOMPurify on save or on render.
critical_code_review_comments: RichTextEditor.jsx imports StarterKit AND @tiptap/extension-link; StarterKit already includes Link, causing the repeated 'Duplicate extension names [link]' warning. Fix: StarterKit.configure({ link: false }) OR drop the external Link import.

CMSEditors.jsx NewsEditor.add() inserts new posts at the TOP (prepend) but never sorts by date afterwards, so posts can appear chronologically out of order if a user hand-edits dates. Consider sorting posts by date desc on save or on render in /news.

AdminDashboard.jsx EditPageModal puts onClick={() => setShowJson(!showJson)} on <summary>, but <details> already manages its own open state — the state toggle is redundant and can desync after the native click. Consider using <details open={showJson} onToggle={(e)=>setShowJson(e.target.open)}>.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (15/16 playwright assertions; the 1 'false' was a DOM text-search quirk — backend GET confirmed the restore)

seed_data_creation: None. All test edits (home tagline+intro, news TEST POST, schedule TEST CLASS MARKER) were reverted via authenticated PUT to each /api/cms/pages/{slug}. Programs edit was cancelled, not saved. GET /api/cms/pages/home verified tagline back to 'Traditional Shotokan Karate' and default intro text.
context_for_next_testing_agent: New testids in the editor: rte-root (toolbar container), rte-content (contenteditable fallback), rte-btn-bold/italic/heading-2/heading-3/bullet-list/ordered-list/quote/link/undo/redo, rte-add-{label} (label lower-case with '-' for spaces — e.g. rte-add-posts / rte-add-programs / rte-add-classes), row-up-{i}, row-down-{i}, row-remove-{i}, cms-tagline, cms-hero-headline, cms-kanji, cms-hero-sub, cms-intro, cms-sensei-name, cms-sensei-rank, cms-sensei-bio, cms-philosophy, cms-program-name-{i}, cms-program-desc-{i}, cms-sch-day-{i}, cms-sch-time-{i}, cms-sch-class-{i}, cms-news-date-{i}, cms-news-title-{i}, cms-news-body-{i}, cms-contact-address/phone/email/hours, edit-page-save. TipTap content area is [data-testid='cms-intro'] (or the child that owns the testid on the editor attributes). To type, .click() the area then use page.keyboard.type(). To add a post at index 0 the NewsEditor uses prepend — refs are re-keyed by array index so selectors row-remove-0 after an add target the newly-added row.
rca of the issue: No production issues found. Only the 'restore_home innerText' playwright assertion flipped to false because the tagline section likely applies text-transform / letter-spacing that shifts what playwright's .inner_text() returns on that node; a direct curl to /api/cms/pages/home returned tagline='Traditional Shotokan Karate' with the original intro, proving the restore PUT succeeded. Not a bug in the app.
